revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Iwaki-jinja]][[ja:石城神社]] {{Expand Japanese|石城神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Iwaki Shrine | native_name = 石城神社 | image = Iwaki-jinja, shaden.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Iwaki Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|33.987405|132.03568}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|az|Ooyamatsimu|de|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|fa|اویاماتسومی|fr|Ōyamatsumi|hr|O-Yama-Tsu-mi|ja|オオヤマツミ|ko|오야마쓰미노카미|mni|ꯑꯣꯌꯃꯠꯁꯨꯃꯤ|ru|Ооямацуми|simple|Ōyamatsumi|sv|Ōyamatsumi|zh|大山津見|qid=Q386563|1=Ōyamatsumi}} }} {{nihongo|'''Iwaki Shrine'''|石城神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Suo Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11416958|ltq=Q11416958|qid=Q11416958|1=List of Shikinaisha in Suo Province}} ** Series ordinal: 2 ** P155: {{ill|Kumakeno Shrine|lt=Kumakeno Shrine|qid=Q135041254|1=Kumakeno Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q11572731|ltq=Q11572731|qid=Q11572731|1=Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q823502|ltq=Q823502|qid=Q823502|1=Hikari, Yamaguchi}} * {{ill|Suō Province|lt=Suō Province|cs|Provincie Suó|de|Provinz Suō|en|Suō Province|es|Provincia de Suō|fa|ولایت سوئو|fr|Province de Suō|gl|Provincia de Suō|id|Provinsi Suō|it|Suō (provincia)|ja|周防国|ko|스오국|lt|Suo žemė|nl|Suo (provincie)|pt|Província de Suō|ru|Суо (провинция)|simple|Suō Province|sk|Suó (provincia)|sr|Провинција Суо|tr|Suō (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Суо|uz|Suo viloyati|wuu|周防国|zh_min_nan|Suô-kok|zh|周防國|qid=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}} * {{ill|Q1042808|ltq=Q1042808|qid=Q1042808|1=Kumage District, Yamaguchi}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|33.987405|132.03568}} == image (P18) == * Iwaki-jinja, shaden.JPG == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q386563|ltq=Q386563|qid=Q386563|1=Ōyamatsumi}} == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Q135037878|ltq=Q135037878|qid=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20160306093022/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/500402.html == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 183124 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q90139226|ltq=Q90139226|qid=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank|ja|正五位|ko|정5위|zh|正五位|qid=Q11123280|1=Senior Fifth Rank}} * {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|ko|종4위|zh|從四位|qid=Q11071127|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} == country (P17) == * Мастор|my|ဂျပန်နိုင်ငံ|mzn|جاپون|nap|Giappone|nds_nl|Japan|nds|Japan|ne|जापान|new|जापान|nia|Jepang|nl|Japan|nlnews|Categorie:Japan|nlvoyage|Japan|nn|Japan|nov|Japan|no|Japan|nqo|ߗ߭ߊߔߐ߲߫|nrm|Japon|nr|Japane|nso|Japane|nv|Binááʼádaałtsʼózí Dineʼé ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * いわきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5250005005675 == Commons category (P373) == * Iwaki-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '山口県光市大字塩田2233', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '山口県光市塩田', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Iwaki-jinja}} [[JA:石城神社]] {{wikidata link|Q24871378}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Ishiki Shrine |Image = [[File:Iwaki-jinja, shaden.JPG|280px]]
Shrine building
{{small| (Main shrine (national {{ill|lt=Important cultural property|Q1188622|qid=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) on the left, worship hall on the right)}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|qid=Q127264|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hikari City|Q823502|qid=Q823502|1=Hikari, Yamaguchi}}2233 Oaza Shioda |Position = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ooyamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Oyama Migami|1=Ōyamatsumi}}
{{ill|lt=Raijin|Q12808082|qid=Q12808082|1=Raijin}}
{{ill|lt=Nakamikami|Q644201|qid=Q644201|lt=Takakami|1=Kuraokami}} |Divinity = |Company rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
Old {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}} |Foundation = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|qid=Q316637|1=Emperor Bidatsu}}3 years ({{ill|lt=574 years|Q32597|qid=Q32597|1=574}}?) |Main shrine = Sumikiri {{ill|lt=Kasugazo|Q3193781|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=October 7th|Q2926|qid=Q2926|1=October 7}}{{Sfn|Ishiki Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1980}} |Shinto ritual =|Map = {{Location map|Japan Yamaguchi|label=Ishiki Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} [[File:Mt Iwaki (Yamaguchi), enkei.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrine enshrined{{ill|lt=Ishijoyama|Q11585424|qid=Q11585424|1=Mount Iwaki (Yamaguchi)}}}}]] '''Iwaki Shrine'' (Iwaki Shrine) is located in {{ill|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|Q127264|qid=Q127264|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hikari City|Q823502|qid=Q823502|1=Hikari, Yamaguchi}} Oaza Shioda. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} and {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Old company rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural company|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha}}. It is located at the top of {{ill|lt=Ishishiroyama|Q11585424|qid=Q11585424|1=Mount Iwaki (Yamaguchi)}} (362 meters above sea level), which is known for {{ill|lt=Ancient Mountain Castle|Q6431764|qid=Q6431764|1=ancient mountain fortress in Japan}}'s {{ill|lt=Ishishiroyama Kamagoishi|Q18990554|qid=Q18990554|1=Iwakisan Kōgoishi}} (national {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|qid=Q1081138|1=Historic site}}). === Enshrined God === The following three deities are currently enshrined. . * {{ill|lt=Oyamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Oyamazumi no Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} - The main deity. * {{ill|lt=Raijin|Q12808082|qid=Q12808082|1=Raijin}} (Ikazuchinokami) * {{ill|lt=Takaokamikami|Q644201|qid=Q644201|lt=Takaokamikami|1=Kuraokami}} In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, which was established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}), there is one enshrined deity. In the past, the enshrined deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=honjibutsu|1=UNKNOWN}} was {{ill|lt=Shakyamuni Buddha|Q11644537|qid=Q11644537|1=Shakyamuni Tathāgata}} for Oyamazumi, and {{jalink|Fugen Bosatsu|Q868306} for thunder god. }, {{ill|lt=Manjushri Bodhisattva|Q471696|qid=Q471696|1=Manjushri}} was enshrined in the Takaojin Shrine.{{Sfn|Ishiki Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1980}} === History === === Founding === [[File:Iwaki-san Kogoishi, higashi-suimon.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|lt=Ishikiyama Kamagoishi|Q18990554|qid=Q18990554|1=Iwakisan Kōgoishi}} (national {{ill|lt=historic site|Q1081138|qid=Q1081138|1=Historic site}})}}]] Founding date is unknown {{Sfn|Ishiki Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1980}}. According to the shrine's legend, it was founded in the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|qid=Q316637|1=Emperor Bidatsu}} ({{ill|lt=574|Q32597|qid=Q32597|1=574}}?) when Tsushi of Kibitunkura came to this area and was given an imperial plaque of ``Ishijo Palace.'' At {{ill|lt=Ishishiroyama|Q11585424|qid=Q11585424|1=Mount Iwaki (Yamaguchi)}}, where Ishishiro Shrine is located, it is known that {{ill|lt=Ishijoyama Kamagoishi|Q18990554|qid=Q18990554|1=Iwakisan Kōgoishi}}, which is an {{ill|lt=ancient mountain castle|Q6431764|qid=Q6431764|1=ancient mountain fortress in Japan}}, was built. There is no mention of the Ishishiro Mountain Kamago Stone in the literature, and its functional period is not clear, but it is presumed that there was a connection between the shrine and the mountain castle, based on the shrine's name, ``Ishijo''. There is also a theory that the shrine outside the precincts, Uwanari Shrine, was originally the god of Mt. Ishishiro, and with the construction of the mountain castle, the gods Oyamazumi, Raijin, and Takashiro were solicited by the imperial court, and Ishishiro Shrine was built as the guardian deity of the mountain castle in place of Uwanari Shrine.{{Sfn|Ishiki Shrine (Gods)|2000}} === Outline === In {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|qid=Q1151168|lt=Kokukushi|1=Rikkokushi}}, {{ill|lt=Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōkan|1=Jōgan}} It is recorded that in the 9th year ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|qid=Q30347|1=867}}), the {{ill|lt=jinka|Q11591025|qid=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} of ``Ishijo no Kami'' was promoted from the upper rank of upper fifth rank to lower rank of junior fourth rank {{Sfn|Ishijo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. Also, {{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=extended|1=Enchō}}5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shinmeicho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} is {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|qid=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}}{{ill|lt=Kumage District (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|Q1042808|qid=Q1042808|lt=Ishiki Shrine is listed in Kumage District|1=Kumage District, Yamaguchi}} and is listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinai Shrine|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} {{Sfn|Ishiki Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}. {{ill|lt=Karoku|Q1194421|qid=Q1194421|1=Karoku}} In March 3rd year ({{ill|lt=1227|Q5407|qid=Q5407|1=1227}}), in ``Suo Ishijogu Shinkan Kai'', we can see a dispute regarding the shrine {{Sfn|Ishijo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}.{{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|qid=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} In July of the first year ({{ill|lt=1469|Q6566|qid=Q6566|1=1469}}), the current main shrine was built by {{ill|lt=Masahiro Ouchi|Q8081786|qid=Q8081786|1=Ōuchi Masahiro}}. This main shrine was built by Yoshioki Ouchi|Q909270}} in {{ill|lt=Eisho|Q1193577|qid=Q1193577|1=Eishō (Muromachi period)}}11 ({{ill|lt=1514|Q6204|qid=Q6204|1=1514}}) and by {{ill|lt=Meireki|Q1192766|qid=Q1192766|1=Meireki}}2 ({{ill|lt=1656|Q6982|qid=Q6982|1=1656}}). link|Tsunahiro Mori|Q11547682}} and {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|qid=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} in 10 years ({{ill|lt=1798|Q7823|qid=Q7823|1=1798}}) and by {{ill|lt=Sarifusa Mori|Q11547649|qid=Q11547649|1=Mōri Narifusa}} respectively. In addition, in {{ill|lt=Ansei|Q1327259|qid=Q1327259|1=Ansei}}4 ({{ill|lt=1857|Q7680|qid=Q7680|1=1857}}), {{ill|lt=Takachika Mori|Q1088423|qid=Q1088423|1=Mōri Takachika}} ​​built the worship hall and Jingoji Niomon (current Ishijo Shrine Zuishinmon). In {{ill|lt=early modern times|Q5308718|qid=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}}, there was a belief that Mt. Ishishiro was a sacred mountain site, and it was called ``Ishijoyama Shikinai Sansha Daigongen'' {{Sfn|Ishijo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}.After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|qid=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}}), {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} It was ranked as {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}} in {{ill|lt=Taisho|Q329174|qid=Q329174|1=Taishō era}}2 ({{ill|lt=1913|Q2080|qid=Q2080|1=1913}}){{Sfn|Ishiki Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1980}}. Additionally, on the west side of the shrine there used to be Ishishiro Sanshanain Jingoji Temple, {{ill|lt=Bettoji|Q11396755|qid=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}, but it was abandoned in 1870|1980}}. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jokan|1=Jōgan}} August 16, 9th year ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|qid=Q30347|1=867}}), from the top of the fifth rank to the bottom of the fourth rank (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - Written as ``Ishijogami.'' === Precincts === [[File:Iwaki-jinja (Hikari), honden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main shrine (National Important Cultural Property)}}]]The current main shrine was built in the first year of {{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|qid=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} ({{ill|lt=1469|Q6566|qid=Q6566|1=1469}}) in the late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|qid=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. It is a large corner-wooden {{ill|lt=Kasugazukuri|Q3193781|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} building. The structure is 3-ken in girder rows, 1-ken in the front, 2-ken in the back, and a gable, and the roof is shingled. Characteristics of the Muromachi period can be seen everywhere, such as the toad. Inside the building, the back room is the inner hall, and the front two rooms are the outer hall, with the inner hall being one step higher. This main shrine is designated as a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|qid=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}, along with one designated palace and two ridge plates.[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=20009&pid=ss&search_value=%E7%9F%B3%E5 %9F%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE&loopcnt_b=0&loopcnt_m=0&loopcnt=0&shicyouson=&meisyou=&shitei=&kubun=&syurui=&jidai=&loopcnt= Main Hall of Ishiki Shrine] (Yamaguchi Prefecture homepage ``Cultural Properties of Yamaguchi Prefecture'', Overview of Cultural Properties). . Regarding the architectural style of the main shrine, there is a document that says it is ``Sumikiri Kasuga-zukuri'' "National Treasures and Important Cultural Properties Encyclopedia 11 Buildings Vol. 1" supervised by the Agency for Cultural Affairs (Mainichi Shimbunsha, 1998), p. 354 and a document that says ``Front irimoya-zukuri, rear gable-zukuri, tsumui-zukuri'' [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/157775 Cultural Heritage Online], but both refer to essentially the same format. ``Sumiki-iri Kasuga-zukuri'' is a type of Kasuga-zukuri (a type of gabled shrine building with an eave attached to the front), in which a sumiki (a member that extends at a 45-degree angle to the main building) is inserted between the main building and the eave.To the west of the precincts, there was once Jingo-ji Temple, a Betto-ji temple {{Sfn|Ishiki Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1980}}. At the end of the Edo period, the headquarters of the {{ill|lt=Second Kiheitai|Q11602283|qid=Q11602283|1=Second Kiheitai}} was also located at Jingo-ji Temple, but Jingo-ji Temple was abandoned in 1870 and the hall was lost, and the remaining Nio-mon gate on the approach is now used as the Zuishin-mon Gate of Ishijo Shrine. File:Iwaki-jinja (Hikari), entrance.JPG|Torii in the precincts File:Jingoji-ato (Hikari), hondo-ato.JPG|Jingoji Temple Main Hall Ruins File:Iwaki-jinja, zuishin-mon.JPG|Zuishinmon (formerly Jingoji Niomon) File:Iwaki-jinja (Hikari), torii.JPG|Torii at the foot of the mountain === Setsusha === [[File:Iwaki-jinja (Hikari), Suga-sha.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Sugasha}}]] There are the following two Sessue Shrines {{Sfn|Ishiki Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1980}}. * Suga Shrine - Precinct Shrine. * Uwanarisha - outside shrine. === Cultural assets === === Important cultural property (designated by the country) === * Main shrine (1 attached palace, 2 ridge tags) (building) *: On May 27, 1907, it was designated as a special protected building based on the {{ill|lt=Ancient Shrines and Temples Preservation Law|Q11411552|qid=Q11411552|1=Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Law}}, and in 1950, it was designated as a national important cultural property with the enactment of the {{ill|lt=Cultural Properties Protection Law|Q1188502|qid=Q1188502|1=Cultural Properties Protection Law}}. Attached properties were additionally designated on February 3, 1979 {{Nationally designated cultural properties database|102|3238|Ishiki Shrine Main Hall}}[http://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/summary.asp?mid=20009&pid=ss&svalue=%E7%9 F%B3%E5%9F%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE&bloop=0&mloop=0&floop=0&shicyouson=&meisyou=&shitei=&kubun=&syurui=&jidai=&loopcnt=&m_mode=&m_value=&m_loopcnt= Main Hall of Ishiki Shrine] (Yamaguchi Prefecture homepage ``Cultural Properties of Yamaguchi Prefecture'', Cultural Properties Directory). [https://www.city.hikari.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunka/bunkazaiitiran.html#kuni Designated Cultural Property > Ishiki Shrine Main Hall] (Hikari City homepage). . === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490360|ref={{Harvid|Ishiki Shrine (Heibonsha)|1980}}}} * Others ** {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Meiji Shrine Shrine)|1980}}}}** {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|qid=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}edited|author=Akira Ito|year=2000|chapter=Ishiki Shrine|title=Japanese Gods -Shrines and Sacred Sites- 2 Sanyo/Shikoku |publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|qid=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=978-4560025024|ref={{Harvid|Ishiki Shrine (Gods)|2000}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Ishijoyama|Q11585424|qid=Q11585424|1=Mount Iwaki (Yamaguchi)}} * {{ill|lt=Ishijoyama Kamagoishi|Q18990554|qid=Q18990554|1=Iwakisan Kōgoishi}} === External link === {{Commonscat}}
